compiled_state.title: As a fix-bug workflow, I can count primary gate signals and route to auto-fix, quick dialog, or epic escalation proportionally
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Wire all classification gates (1a, 1b, 2a, 2b, 2c, 2d, 3a) together with escalation math that counts primary signals and routes proportionally: 0 signals → auto-fix, 1 signal → quick dialog (1-2 questions), 2+ signals → escalate to /dso:brainstorm, COMPLEX → always escalate.
**Why**: The gates are independently valuable but the escalation routing is what turns individual signals into proportional action. Without this integration layer, gates fire without consequences.
**Scope**:
- IN: Signal counting across all primary gates (1b, 2a, 2c, 2d); blast radius modifier enrichment of dialog text (2b); complexity evaluator COMPLEX override (3a); auto-fix path (0 signals); quick dialog format (1 signal, 1-2 questions); epic escalation path (2+ signals or COMPLEX); integration with debug-everything's interactivity flag from Epic A
- OUT: Individual gate implementations (Stories 1-5); modifying gate signal semantics

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, when zero primary gates fire, the fix proceeds autonomously without user interaction
  ← Satisfies: "When zero primary gates fire, the fix proceeds autonomously"
- When this story is complete, when exactly one primary gate fires, fix-bug prompts a 1-2 question inline dialog with blast radius context if available
  ← Satisfies: "When exactly one primary gate fires, fix-bug prompts a 1-2 question inline dialog"
- When this story is complete, when two or more primary gates fire, the bug is escalated to /dso:brainstorm for epic treatment
  ← Satisfies: "When two or more primary gates fire, the bug is escalated to /dso:brainstorm for epic treatment"
- When this story is complete, the complexity evaluator returning COMPLEX always escalates to epic regardless of other signals
  ← Satisfies: "The complexity evaluator returning COMPLEX always escalates to epic"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Escalation math is the integration point for all gates — changes to any gate's signal semantics propagate here; consider a gate registry pattern
- [Testing] Test matrix should cover all signal combinations: 0, 1, 2+ signals with and without blast radius modifier, with and without COMPLEX override

comments.body: Consideration: [Integration] The interactivity flag from Epic A (2df5-72cb) has no interface contract defined yet. Define expected flag shape and default behavior (assume interactive) when Epic A has not been deployed.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Done definition update: Story 6 consumes all gate signals conforming to the schema defined in Story 0. Integration tests verify all gates produce compatible output.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Consideration: [Reliability] Missing gate signals should default to 0 (no concern) rather than runtime error, enabling partial deployment and independent story verification.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: All 4 child tasks completed: escalation router script, RED tests, and SKILL.md integration.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
